opp.name: BMI_S2C_2025
opp.next_step: June 25 2026: answers to your questions sent by Caroline June 19: Demo was well received, follow up items shared by Venkat June 3, 2026: Caroline sent answers to latest questions and 2 commercials for the Core Modules & Core + Optional Modules May 28, 2026: Follow up call on commercials and supplier network 08/05/26: RfP submitted today
opp.ais_why:
• Dec 2025 Avoma notes from a meeting with BMI's Venkat capture an explicit Zycus action item to 'provide additional materials on Generative AI in procurement and use-case examples from existing clients', plus discussion of Merlin and Teams-based intake. The customer-side ask for GenAI procurement materials and use-case examples is a buyer-originated AI signal, not Zycus rep-led positioning. Merlin and Merlin Intake are in deal scope alongside iSource, iRisk and iContract.
• Formal Evaluation stage with BD Engaged. Venkat is preparing an internal business case for year-end with budget approval pending; commercial discussion captured pricing of USD $220K/yr + $380-400K implementation. ROI workshop and RFP template have been requested by the customer. Last activity May 2026. The deal momentum is anchored in BMI's internal budget-approval cycle ahead of formal RFP.
• High confidence: SF Description was null but Avoma fills the gap decisively with GenAI follow-up requests and an engaged economic buyer (Venkat) preparing a business case. Held at 7 (not 8+) because customer's primary questions are still ROI/efficiency-focused rather than an explicit AI mandate or POC. A buyer-stated 'AI as selection criterion' would push to 8-9.

sources: Avoma demo 2026-06-17 (Venkata Masetty on 40k suppliers, multi-ERP)
SF Products__c (full S2C suite incl. Merlin)
metrics.narrative: BMI articulated large-scale operational pain on the 17 Jun demo: roughly 40,000 suppliers spread across SAP and non-SAP ERPs with likely duplicates that need consolidation, de-duplication and hierarchy mapping ('we have roughly 40,000 of them spread in different ERPs, SAP and non-SAP environment, so they potentially have duplicates', Venkata Masetty, 2026-06-17), plus ESG/compliance gating and cross-functional risk (SCAR) needs. None of this is yet quantified: there is no cost-of-doing-nothing, cycle-time, headcount, or savings figure in Salesforce, Next Step, or the call. Venkat is preparing an internal business case with budget approval pending (AIS_Why), so co-building a numbers-backed ROI he can defend is the highest-leverage metrics move before proposal finalization.

sources: Next Step (follow-up items shared by Venkat 06-03/06-19)
champion.narrative: Venkata Masetty (Group Category Manager - Coatings) is the confirmed champion and internal project lead for the Supplier Integrated Platform. He set and owned the demo agenda, drove the most detailed use-case questioning, is the primary Zycus contact across the RFP cycle, and is authoring the internal business case for budget approval. His advocacy is active in driving the evaluation, but there are still gaps: he has not shared competitive intel, has not introduced Zycus to the budget signatory, and his engagement remains largely responsive to Zycus asks rather than proactively selling internally. He has access to procurement leadership (Sharon Moar, Jeremy Dussel) who attend his sessions. Converting him from evaluation-driver to internal seller, armed with a quantified ROI, is the key champion move.

sources: Prior canonical record (competitive landscape unknown, carried forward)
competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is blank, and no rival is mentioned in Next Step, tasks, or the 17 Jun demo transcript (Caroline's prior tenure 'at Coupa' is her own background, not a BMI alternative, and must not be read as a competitor). For a formal, RFP-led $180k enterprise S2C deal a multi-vendor shortlist is highly likely, so the absence of intel is a blind spot rather than evidence of a sole source. Per living memory this stays an open unknown carried forward, not a fabricated name. Surfacing who else is in BMI's RFP shortlist, directly from Venkat, is a required move so we can position Merlin's agentic risk and contract capabilities against the real field.

sources: Avoma demo 2026-06-17 (Venkata Masetty, Julia Alenina, Elizaveta on ESG/gating/40k suppliers)
identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clearly articulated and cross-functional. BMI runs ~40,000 suppliers across fragmented SAP and non-SAP ERPs with duplicate risk requiring consolidation and hierarchy mapping (Venkata Masetty, 2026-06-17); wants strict ESG/compliance gating so onboarding cannot proceed on incomplete data; needs consolidated multi-source risk views (internal assessments plus D&B/EcoVadis/sanctions) clustered by risk theme such as forced labour and human rights; and needs cross-functional SCAR handling across procurement, quality and R&D. Bank-account fraud control via Trustpair validation is a named requirement. The pain owners are Venkata Masetty (project lead) and the P2P process owners; the pain is confirmed and broad but not yet attached to a financial number.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are only partly visible. A legal representative joined the demo and signalled BMI contracts both under formal contracts and under terms and conditions, and BMI stressed data handling and configurable validation gates, so an MSA/SOW plus legal review is expected. Zycus positioned its own e-signature (Certinal) with Docusign/Adobe Sign options and iSaaS middleware for ERP integration. Not yet known: NDA/MSA status, InfoSec review timeline, procurement channel, and whether legal runs in parallel with commercials. The 40k-supplier, multi-ERP migration plus a buyer concern that the implementation timeline 'seems very long' (Caroline, 2026-05-04) means implementation and contracting scope could extend the paper process well past 31 Jul; confirming the contracting and InfoSec path is the move.

sources: Avoma demo 2026-06-17 (Jeremy Dussel, Sharon Moar attended)
AIS_Why (budget approval pending, Venkat business case)
economic_buyer.narrative: The budget owner is not confirmed. Every OpportunityContactRole carries a blank Role, and the person controlling the commercial decision has not been named on a call. Venkata Masetty leads the project and is building the internal business case, but at Group Category Manager - Coatings he is the driver, not the signatory. Jeremy Dussel (Procurement Director, processes and analytics) and Sharon Moar (Director, Procurement Excellence) both attended the demo and are the most senior procurement voices in the room, making one of them the likely budget-approval path, but neither has been confirmed as the economic buyer. Mapping who signs the budget behind Venkat's business case is a ranked move before the proposal goes up.

sources: Next Step / activity history (RFP 05-08, commercials 06-03, demo 06-17, answers 06-25)
decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, BMI is running a formal RFP-led evaluation: RFP submitted 2026-05-08, Q&A rounds through April-May, commercials shared 2026-06-03, then a structured 2-hour solution demo on 2026-06-17 with a pre-shared agenda and use cases. The demo drew a broad committee: category management (Venkata Masetty, Julia Alenina), procurement leadership (Sharon Moar, Jeremy Dussel), IT (Estela Barbero), plus P2P and legal voices, indicating a cross-functional buying group. The forward gates are internal business-case approval, budget sign-off, finalist selection, and contracting, but the finalist-decision date and approval chain are not yet documented. The 2026-06-25 answers email leaves the ball with the buyer, so pinning the decision timeline is the next process step.

sources: Avoma demo 2026-06-17 (use-case walkthrough and buyer questions)
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit from the RFP and the use-case-driven demo. BMI is scoring on: supplier onboarding with self-service plus BMI-initiated paths and duplicate prevention; multi-language support ('19 plus languages', regional defaults and supplier-chosen language); configurable workflows, gating and prequalification ('we cannot go further with the supplier onboarding if some steps has not been completed', Venkata Masetty, 2026-06-17); third-party risk apps (Trustpair, D&B, EcoVadis) and Merlin risk radar; ESG/human-rights clustering dashboards; SCAR and supplier development; contract lifecycle with Merlin Insta Review, bulk extract and multi-language executive summaries; and ERP integration via iSaaS. A hard, buyer-stated criterion is platform fit with the relevant stakeholder, not Teams ('we don't work on Teams, we work with the relevant stakeholder, that's something very important when we discuss implementation').

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS 7 (Moderate) is corroborated by the calls: BMI asked specifically what AI types Zycus uses (generative, agentic, predictive, copilot) and engaged deeply on Merlin risk radar and contract AI, but the primary lens remains ROI/efficiency rather than an explicit AI mandate or POC, consistent with the held-at-7 read.

No rival is named across Competitors__c, Next Step, tasks, or the 17 Jun demo despite a formal RFP for a full-suite $180k S2C deal, so a multi-vendor shortlist is likely but Zycus has zero intel on the finalists. This is the single biggest blind spot at 28 days to close; the move is to get the shortlist from Venkat, not to invent a competitor.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: BMI Group — Formal Evaluation · $180K · closes 31 Jul

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 28 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 51 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 5 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 20 is anchored on the stage win (28), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 51, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 5 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

deal_movement.summary: Live, two-way and moving through June: RFP submitted 05-08, commercials sent 06-03, a well-received 2-hour demo on 06-17, buyer praise on 06-19, and answers sent 06-25 (rep-initiated, awaiting buyer reply). Close date was pulled in from 2026-12-31 to 2026-07-31 on 04-07. Momentum is real but the buyer has not yet crossed a finalist-selection or budget-approval milestone.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer engagement is live and warm: a well-received 2-hour demo, unsolicited praise on 19 Jun, and a broad committee in the room. The only cooling signal is that the ball has sat with the buyer since the 25 Jun answers with no confirmed next step.

north_star_verdict.math: 28 days to close (close 2026-07-31, today 2026-07-03). Formal Evaluation, deal created 2025-08-13 (~325 days). RFP submitted 2026-05-08 (56 days ago), commercials (Core and Core+Optional) sent 2026-06-03, solution demo 2026-06-17 well received, follow-up answers sent 2026-06-25 (rep-initiated, awaiting buyer reply). Close date was pulled IN from 2026-12-31 to 2026-07-31 on 2026-04-07, an optimistic move. Remaining path (finalist selection, quantified business case, budget sign-off, MSA/SOW, InfoSec, ERP-integration scoping, signature) cannot realistically complete in 28 days, especially with Venkat's business case targeted at a later budget-approval cycle. Deal is genuinely healthy and engaged; the sole real problem is the date, so this reads Close Date Risk, not Slowing.
evidence: Solution demo 2026-06-17 (Avoma), 2-hour multi-stakeholder session across iSupplier, iRisk, iContract, iSource with deep buyer questioning
Buyer email 2026-06-19: 'very productive session' and specific commendation of SC Asmita Mukherjee
Commercials for Core Modules and Core+Optional sent 2026-06-03 per Next Step
Answers to buyer questions sent 2026-06-25 (rep-initiated, awaiting buyer reply)
Budget approval pending; Venkat preparing an internal business case (AIS_Why)
Economic buyer / budget owner not confirmed on any OpportunityContactRole (all roles blank)
No quantified ROI or business case documented
No competitor named in any source despite a formal RFP
north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, highly engaged $180k full-suite S2C deal: 2-hour multi-stakeholder demo on 17 Jun was well received (buyer praised the SC on 19 Jun), commercials shared, answers sent 25 Jun. But at 28 days to close the buyer is still building an internal business case with budget approval pending, no finalist selection, and the economic buyer unmapped, so the 31 Jul date is not credible and will slip.

flags: Map the budget signatory behind Venkat's business case (likely Jeremy Dussel or Sharon Moar) and get Zycus in the room before the proposal goes up.
Ask Venkat directly who else is in BMI's RFP shortlist; a formal RFP with zero competitive intel is the deal's biggest blind spot.
Own the IT and legal threads: Estela Barbero on ERP-integration scope and the legal contact on data governance, to de-risk the 40k-supplier multi-ERP implementation.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers at 28 days to close: map the budget signatory, get the competitive shortlist, and lock the IT/legal and implementation-scope threads.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape unknown and carried forward from prior sweeps. No rival is named across Competitors__c, Next Step, tasks, or the 17 Jun demo despite a formal RFP for a full-suite $180k S2C deal, so a multi-vendor shortlist is likely but Zycus has zero intel on the finalists. This is the single biggest blind spot at 28 days to close; the move is to get the shortlist from Venkat, not to invent a competitor.

The main exposures are platform-environment fit (Google Workspace, not Teams) and a long implementation timeline for a 40k-supplier multi-ERP migration.
